title: sections data model and migration
description: Add the `sections` table (`id, titulo, ordem`) to the Drizzle schema and generate the migration.
acceptanceCriteria: `sections` table exists with `id (text/uuid pk), titulo (text not null), ordem (integer not null)`
A unique or check constraint prevents two sections from sharing the same `ordem` value if strict ordering is desired (or ordering is resolved at the application layer — document the chosen approach)
Migration applies cleanly on top of the Fase 0/1 schema
Drizzle query layer exposes typed insert/select helpers for `sections`

steps.details: In `apps/server/src/db/schema.ts`, add `export const sections = sqliteTable('sections', { id: text('id').primaryKey(), titulo: text('titulo').notNull(), ordem: integer('ordem').notNull() })`.
Run `drizzle-kit generate` to produce the new migration SQL file. Review it to confirm only the `sections` table is added, with no unintended changes to existing tables.

Choose whether `ordem` values must be unique (enforced by a unique index) or may have gaps/ties resolved by insertion order as a tiebreaker at the application layer. Document the decision in a code comment near the schema definition.

steps.description: Verify migration against a fresh database
steps.details: Apply the migration to a fresh `DATABASE_PATH`, insert a few sections via a Drizzle query, and confirm they read back with correct `ordem` values.

technicalNotes: Keep `ordem` as a plain integer with application-level renumbering on reorder (simplest for MVP scale) rather than a fractional-index scheme — revisit only if reordering performance becomes a real problem.
